entries.name: Abyss
entries.summary: Abyss is a type of encounter introduced in Rise of the Abyssal league that can be encountered in acts and maps. It is first encounterable in Mastodon Badlands or Valley of the Titans.
mechanics: Fissures can be found randomly in areas near packs of monsters. Defeating monsters nearby will cause the fissures to close, causing Abyssal monsters to spawn. Closing several clustered fissures will cause a pit to open, releasing larger and more dangerous monsters. Monsters spawned by the pit absorb the monster rarity…
Abyssal Troves are magic or rare rarity chests containing random rewards and preserved bones that can also come in other variants with specific rewards:
Abyssal Armoury: Contains only armour items and rib preserved bones
Abyssal Arsenal: Contains only weapon items and jawbone preserved bones
Abyssal Coffer: Contains only currency
If a map has the modifier Area is overrun by the Abyssal desecrated modifier, it will cause all natural inhabitants of the map to spawn near Abysses.
If a map has the modifier Abysses lead to an Abyssal Boss desecrated modifier, it will cause the final Abyssal Pit to lead to either Lightless Void or Dark Domain.
In the campaign, repeatable Abyss encounters can be found in Mastodon Badlands, Valley of the Titans, and The Venom Crypts.
— Interactions with other encounters —

Other encounters and mechanics can interact with abysses. These effects only occur if the encounters naturally spawned near the abyss. Bringing a distant monster to an abyss before killing it will not cause it to have these extra mechanics.

entries.name: Breach
entries.summary: Breach is a type of map encounter. Breaches are tears in reality that summon Hiveborn monsters from another dimension.
mechanics: Initially, only Unstable Breaches can be found in the wild. Inside a Breach Fortress, you can also find Breach Hive encounters, and larger Hive Fortress encounters near the center of the fortress. Certain Atlas passives can cause Breach Hives to appear in maps outside of Breach Fortresses.
— Unstable Breaches —
Unstable Breaches appear as purple hands emanating energy. When touched, the Breach will begin to expand and summon Hiveborn monsters. Hiveborn monsters are confined within the Breach circle.

When an Unstable Breach is opened, a meter will appear indicating the encounter status, shown as a two overlapping bars. Your goal is to fill up the lighter bar by killing Hiveborn monsters before the brighter section fills up that bar and collapse the Breach prematurely. If you manage the completely fill the bar, whe…
Occasionally, after killing all the Rare monsters, the Breach may summon Vruun, Marshall of Xesht instead of collapsing. If this happens, the Breach will collapse after Vruun is killed.
Once the Breach encounter ends, whether prematurely or not, it will drop items from the Breach encounter at the player's location.
— Breach Hive —

Breach Hives are large stabilised Breaches surrounded by organic walls. When you approach these walls, they will burn away. There are also numerous fissures that burst and burn away walls in a large area, which can detonate nearby fissures to create a chain reaction. At the center of the Breach Hive, there will be a b…

Ailith's health is split into sections, and the amount of damage she takes depends on the power of the enemy that hits her. If Ailith's health is fully depleted, the encounter is failed. Once the encounter begins, walking near the walls will no longer destroy them, but some walls will be randomly destroyed as the wave…
When starting the encounter, multiple purple flames will spawn near Ailith, which can be activated to unleash various abilities to help defend her:

entries.name: Delirium
entries.summary: Delirium is a type of map encounter. Upon approaching a Delirium mirror, the entire zone becomes covered in a grey fog that summons monsters and empowers them. Monsters summoned or empowered by the fog have a chance to drop liquid emotions.
mechanics: Maps that contain a Delirium encounter will spawn a Delirium Mirror near the starting area of the map. Walking through the mirror will cause the Delirium fog to spread outward in a ring shape. While in the fog, additional Delirium monsters will spawn, and existing Magic and Rare monsters may gain Delirium modifiers. T…
The deeper you go into the fog, the more Delirious the encounter will be, causing more monsters to appear, buffing monsters with more damage and additional Toughness, and improving their item drops.

During a Delirium encounter, a meter will appear indicating progress in the encounter; the main bar indicates how much of the map is covered in the fog, the bottom marker shows how deep you are in the fog, and the top markers show how deep you need to go into the fog to find a special Fracturing Mirror. The rightmost…

The Delirium encounter will end if you step out of the fog for more than 5 seconds. Delirium fog is paused temporarily while no players are in the area or while facing certain other encounters or the map boss, indicated by the meter turning black. It is also possible to end the Delirium encounter early by pressing the…
The Delirium fog will flow in the direction of the map boss.
— Fracturing Mirror Shards —

Fracturing Mirror Shards are special types of Mirror Shards that have additional effects. The top part of the meter will show how deep in the fog you need to go to find them, but the type of Mirror Shard that will spawn will be a mystery until it is found.
The effects of the Mirror Shard depend on its color:
Escalation (Blue?): Add an additional Delirium modifier to a Rare monster.
Escalation (Teal?): Temporarily pauses the Delirium fog.

entries.name: Ritual
entries.summary: Rituals are a type of encounter. Rituals are mysterious sacrificial sites built by the The King in the Mists.
mechanics: Zones containing a Ritual encounter spawn 3 or 4 Ritual altars. Ritual altars can be activated once most of the monsters surrounding the altar are killed. Activating a Ritual altar will enclose the area in darkness, preventing the player from leaving, and begins reviving monsters killed within the Ritual circle. The a…

Killing monsters within the circle will grant tribute. The amount of tribute earned from killing the monsters during the encounter depends on the monster's rarity, as well as how many times it has appeared in a ritual in that area; it will provide 25% less tribute each time it is revived. One of the options in the atl…

After a ritual altar is complete, tribute earned can be spent at the altar for rewards. A swarm of locusts will fly out of completed Ritual altars that point in the direction of the nearest incomplete Ritual altar.
The encounter will be marked as completed once all Ritual altars have been found.

It is possible to "drag" monsters (e.g. Rogue Exiles) into the Ritual area before killing them to cause them to be resurrected during the ritual encounter. Similarly, monsters that move out of the area before dying will not be resurrected.

entries.name: Azmerian wisp
entries.summary: Azmerian wisps, and their associated Azmeri Spirits, are a type of encounter. They represent various Azmeri Animal Guardians and appears as a wisp of light that can be one of four varieties: Wild, Vivid, Primal, or Sacred.
mechanics: Wisps appear as a glowing ball of light. As you approach the wisp, it will flee, causing monsters it comes into contact to be Spirit-influenced with the X-Touched effect, granting specific buffs. The wisp moves faster the closer you are, and will slow down if you are too far away. If you remain too far away for a peri…

The wisp will attempt to seek out a rare or unique enemy in the area to possess it, restoring it to full life and granting it the Hunted by X effect which grants enhanced buffs to the monster and periodically summons spiritual animals to aid in combat. The monster will also have more rewarding drops, which also scale…
Wisps will disappear if there is no rare or unique monsters in the area that it can possess.
Wisp of the Spring Hare can appear at area level 33+ and Wisp of the Cunning Fox can appear at area level 66+.

changes: Azmeri Spirits that are released from monsters now retain their power and empower other spirits released from the monster.
Delwyn no longer appears alongside Azmerian Wisps during the Campaign after the first time you encounter him.
Azmeri Spirits now start appearing from Hunting Grounds and beyond, instead of in Grelwood.
Wisp minimap icons are no longer removed from your minimap if you run out of range of them.
Azmeri Spirits of the Spring Hare and Fox are now approximately three times more common.
Rare monsters possessed by Azmerian Wisps now always drop a Rare item.

entries.name: Cleansed map
entries.summary: Cleansed map are a type of map encounter that can appear after completing a Corrupted Nexus. Doing so will convert all nearby maps affected by Coalesced Corruption to instead become cleansed maps, which contain additional cleansed monster packs.
mechanics: Cleansed monsters have a chance to drop a Fracturing Orb.
One possible cleansed map modifier causes the unique monster The Immured Fury to appear in the map. The Immured Fury always drops a Fracturing Orb when killed.

entries.name: Coalesced Corruption
entries.summary: Coalesced Corruption, also known as simply Corruption or Beyond, is a type of map encounter that appears in areas affected by corruption. Monsters slain close together in these areas have a chance to merge together and manifest more powerful monsters.
mechanics: Corrupted areas are more likely to have modifiers. These modifiers are removed if the corruption encounter is removed from a map, such as through failing a map.
Defeating Beyond monsters has a chance to spawn stronger and higher rarity monsters. Each map can have one of three specific Beyond factions.
— Corrupted Nexus —

Several maps with Coalesced Corruption will surround a map indicated as a Corrupted Nexus. The Corrupted Nexus will always contain a Beyond boss that appears after killing the map boss. In order to access a Corrupted Nexus, a waystone with at least 4 modifiers must be used.
Completing the Corrupted Nexus map will cause all nearby maps with Coalesced Corruption to become cleansed maps instead, causing different monsters to appear.

Upon completing your first Corrupted Nexus, it will unlock the Doryani's Science Atlas specialisation. The first three skill point for Doryani's Science are acquired by completing Corrupted Nexuses. A guaranteed Corrupted Nexus will spawn to the southeast of the Ziggurat Encampment.

changes: Unidentified Corrupted Waystones can now be used to access a Corrupted Nexus.
Reduced the number of Corrupted Nexus' you need to complete your Atlas Passive Tree. Each Corrupted Nexus now grants 5 skill points allowing you to complete this task much faster.
Increased the visibility of Corrupted Nexus' so that you can see them from further away.

Areas of Corruption on the Atlas now have a central Corrupted Nexus that is the source of the affliction. In the areas leading to the Nexus, Corruption will spill out from slain enemies and amalgamate into powerful monsters.
Upon reaching the Nexus you will begin the task of cleansing the source of Corruption. Complete this task and receive Crystalline Shards that you can use to obtain Atlas Passive Skill points.
A Boss for each faction has been added, these appear in the Corrupted Nexus.
Additional Corruption Monster factions have also been introduced.
Corruption Monsters have had their visuals and animations improved, and their skills have been rebalanced.
Corruption areas no longer implicitly have +1 to Area Level but now are much more likely to have modifiers, and the modifiers can be more powerful. Some new Corruption modifiers have also been added.

While you have An Audience With the King in your inventory, there will be buttons on the Atlas where you can use the item to reveal the Crux of N…

changes: Followers of the King in the Mists have taken up residence within the maze in the Crux of Nothingness, and aim to kill you as you try to escape the maze.

entries.name: Encounter
entries.summary: An encounter, also known as league mechanic, map mechanic, or extra content, is an instance of content added to an area that offers the user additional challenge (often in the form of additional and more challenging monsters) for a chance at a reward (often items). Some encounter types, like Ritual, are introduced via…

changes: Fate of the Vaal has been added to the Core Game. You first encounter a series of 6 Ancient Beacons in Act 3, energise them to obtain Energised Crystals and open a portal to the Vaal Ruins. You'll encounter a second set of 6 Ancient Beacons in the Interludes.

Abyss has been added to the core game. You can find Abyss encounters in specific campaign areas, starting in Act 2. Abyss encounters have been added to the Endgame, as well as Abyss Tablets and related modifiers.
Map Areas now generate with 1 to 3 extra random content, including Breach, Delirium, Ritual, Expedition, Shrines, Strongboxes, Essences, Wisps, Rogue Exiles, and Summoning Circles.

This content will be added in addition to the content added by your Tablets and Map Modifiers, and selected afterwards. If you have a Modifier that adds an Expedition Encounter, which cannot have more than 1 in an area, a different type of extra content is selected instead.

Introduced 12 Rogue Exiles - others like you who have been exiled and are openly hostile. Each one has their own skills and personalities, and are much smarter than the average monster. These Exiles use unique items that they will drop if you successfully kill them.

Introduced Azmerian Wisps - spirits that can be found throughout both the campaign and the Endgame. As you approach these wisps, they will flee, leading you past monsters. Any monsters it comes into contact with are infused by the spirit, making them more powerful and the spirit…
Extra content, such as Breach, Delirium, Expedition and Ritual, now start spawning further away from The Ziggurat Refuge.
Minimap Icons for League mechanics now remain visible regardless of how far away you travel from them, making it easier to return to them later.

Added the new Overseer's Precursor Tablet, a currency item which can be placed in completed Towers to add Map Bosses to valid Maps in radius. These tablets drop from Map Bosses, and can roll exclusive modifiers that affect Map Bosses.

Map areas have been adjusted to have more distinct specialities for the content they contain. For example, one Map area might have more total number of monsters, while another is more likely to contain additional content such as Strongboxes, Essences or Shrines. Generally speaki…

entries.name: Essence (encounter)
entries.summary: Essences are a type of encounter found in both campaign (as early as Act 1 Hunting Grounds) and endgame maps.
mechanics: Players may occasionally encounter several monsters frozen in crystallised corruption. This always consists of a single rare monster and several normal minions. Clicking on the Essence monolith three times will free the monster, allowing it to fight the player.
The rare Essence monster inherits one or more Essence monster modifiers which correspond to the essences it holds. Once defeated, it will drop each of the listed essences.
Using a Vaal Orb on an imprisoned monster will corrupt it and modify the essences it's holding in unpredictable ways. Outcomes include:
No change.
Add an additional random essence.
Reroll all essences, transforming them into the same tier of essences.
Upgrade essences into their better versions and transform Perfect Essences into a Corrupted Essence.
Essence monsters are freed immediately
Note: If the result is an upgrade or reroll to another type, then Essence of the Abyss will be removed if present.
Essence encounters do not respawn if reset by character death.

entries.name: Rogue exile
entries.summary: Rogue exiles are a special type of monsters. Wandering around Wraeclast, these hostile exiles seek out a fight to the death. Rogue exiles start appearing in Act 4 of the campaign and have an ~8% chance to appear in maps.
mechanics: Rogue exiles are Unique monsters based on playable characters and can use player skills, including Dodge Roll. They use a more advanced AI than other monsters with smart targeting and smart pathfinding. They can also use life flasks to heal themselves mid-combat.

Rogue exiles come equipped with their own set of equipment, which can occasionally include drop-enabled unique items, and stats from these items will affect the rogue exile. They may use items with socketed augments which are retained when dropped. Any unique items they carry will be displayed under their modifiers. W…
A Rogue exile captured in a Ritual generates new equipment each time they are respawned, including unique items.

Rogue exiles that spawn near abysses will respawn as a Lichborn Exile when killed, imbuing them with additional abilities and Abyssal modifiers. Their name will display as " , the Lich Born". Lichborn exiles can drop the exclusive unique item Heart of the Well.

entries.summary: The Simulacrum is a map that can be accessed via Delirium encounters.
access: Upon completing a map with a Grand Mirror, you can add Delirium fog to a map on the Atlas, which will spread to nearby maps. Once the maps in the fog reaches 100% Deliriousness, at least one of the fog covered maps will turn into a Simulacrum.
An itemised Simulacrum (map fragment) can be used to transform a map with 100%+ Deliriousness into a Simulacrum.
mechanics: The Simulacrum consists of 7 waves of enemies to defeat. The map is an amalgamation of interconnected arenas based on tilesets from Act 1. The Deliriousness percentage of the Simulacrum increases with each wave, starting with 100% Deliriousness at wave 1, going up to 200% at wave 7.

Escalating Threats: Adds an additional modifier to the area. These modifiers generally add danger and reward.
Apex Predators: Adds an additional map boss to the encounter.
Pure Emotions: Adds additional monster packs to the encounter.
guaranteedDrops: One Simulacrum-exclusive unique item drops after completing the 7th (final) wave of the simulacrum. Additionally, 1 to 2 Raven's Reflection will also drop.
Assailum — 42% of the guaranteed pool
Perfidy — 33% of the guaranteed pool
Collapsing Horizon — 10% of the guaranteed pool

Strugglescream — 2% of the guaranteed pool

Megalomaniac (3 mod) — 3% of the guaranteed pool
Voices — ~1%? of the guaranteed pool
Tangmazu's Reliquary Key — <1% of the guaranteed pool

entries.name: Strongbox
entries.summary: Strongboxes are an encounter found in both the campaign and endgame. Strongboxes are a type of chest that can have modifiers. These chests may be crafted upon with currency items. When a strongbox is opened, a dark mist will flow out of them and summon packs of monsters. Once all the monsters are slain, the chest will…
mechanics: Strongboxes can have most currency items applied to them to alter its modifiers. The modifiers on a strongbox will add or improve the items it drops or empower monsters within.

Rare Strongboxes can have up to 3 prefixes and suffixes by default. They can have up to 4 prefixes and suffixes after certain Atlas passives have been allocated. In addition, certain Atlas passives will enable certain modifiers to be able to be rolled on strongboxes.
Corrupting a strongbox with a Vaal Orb results in all items being Corrupted.
Certain mechanics may allow a strongbox to be openable again. To do so, the player must hold the left click button down on the strongbox.

The loot from strongboxes is not affected by rarity modifiers from your gear, though they are affected by strongbox-specific modifiers. Strongboxes innately gain 50% more item quantity and rarity starting from Act 4, and 100% more in endgame.

entries.name: The Genesis Tree
entries.summary: The Genesis Tree is a type of crafting mechanic located in the Monastery of the Keepers. Using Hiveblood and wombgifts collected from Breach monsters, the Genesis Tree can birth items. The tree's wombs have a set of passive skills that can be allocated to modify the outcome of the crafts.
mechanics: Crafting with the Genesis Tree requires two components: Hiveblood, which is automatically gained at the end of breach encounters based on Breach monsters killed; and a Wombgift, which can drop at the end of Breach encounters or can be found in breach chests in Hive Fortresses.

The Genesis Tree has four different "wombs", each corresponding to a different reward type: currency, ring, amulet, belt; plus a fifth tree-less womb for crafting Breachstones from Revelatory Wombgifts. Each womb slot accepts only its specific type of wombgift. Once a wombgift is placed in a slot, press the button bel…

Each womb tree has 10 or 15 passive points that can be allocated to them, which is enough to spec only about 1/6th of the total available passive tree nodes. Passives on the Genesis Tree can be freely respecced with no cost.

The Genesis Tree can generate exclusive item bases, as well as catalysts used to add quality to jewellery and jewels. It can also create rings and amulets with exclusive caster or minion explicit modifiers.
All collected Hiveblood with automatically go to the Genesis Tree. The Genesis Tree can hold up to 100,000 Hiveblood.
Holding Ctrl while left clicking the "Use Wombgift" button will change the cursor to quick-use other wombgifts by left-clicking them.

Each of the four wombs has 10 or 15 passive points each that must be unlocked, separately from unlocking the ability to assign the passive tree nodes. On the Breach Atlas Passive Tree, Diverse Control grants 2 points for each womb, Exquisite Design grants 5 points for the Amulet womb, and Growing Wealth grants 5 point…

entries.name: Verisium Remnant
entries.summary: Verisium Remnants, also known as Runic Remnants or simply Remnants, are a type of encounter. They can be found as individual encounters in Runes of Aldur league, and can also be found as part of Expedition encounters.
mechanics: Verisium Remnants appear in the form of a large chunk of rock inscribed with runes, guarded by a pack of monsters. Pulsing blue Verisium veins can be found in the terrain nearby. Once the nearby monsters are killed, the remnant can be interacted with, revealing the number of runes it can be inscribed with one of its s…

Clicking on the "Runeshape Combinations" icon will open an interface that lets you inscribe a runeshape combination into the remnant, allowing the player to choose a specific reward to receive from the remnant encounter, as well as the list of modifiers it will add to the encounter. The available options will depend o…

Once the runeshape combination have been chosen, the remnant can be activated, raising nearby defeated monsters and summoning new ones while also empowering them or unleashing skills. Longer runeshape combinations will spawn additional waves of monsters. The remnant will apply the rune modifiers to summoned monsters i…
Completing a Verisium Remnant encounter for the first time will unlock the Runeshape Tome interface, which can be opened with by default.
— Expedition —

Remnants can also be found as part of Expedition encounters. Detonated remnants will apply the effect of a random rune in its runeshape combination to monsters in subsequent explosion in the explosion chain. Remnants must be fully inscribed before detonating them.

changes: Remnants now randomly choose which Rune slot will propagate to further Monsters in Expeditions and Grand Expeditions, rather than randomly which Rune will propagate based on each recipe.
Added a highlight to the Rune that will be propagated upon completing a Remnant in the Runeshape Recipes list.
The maximum number of Remnants in a Grand Expedition now increases per Waystone Tier, with the highest number of Remnants possible to be found in Tier 15+ Maps.
The Runic Modifiers on Monsters granted by Runic Inscriptions now improve the rewards of the Monsters more significantly. These have in most cases been doubled, in some cases significantly more.
Introduced to the game.

notes: Remnant encounters drop Verisium and other crafting components used for Runeforging.
Specific runeshape combinations can grant exclusive runes, alloys, and fluxes. Some of these items are unlocked after completing Farrow's quest in specific Acts.
